description: A designed vertical 'Case Spotlight' series cover/title card. The background is a dark blue gritty textured wall. From the top center hangs a black tactical flashlight (held by a lanyard/cord) angled downward, casting a bright white triangular beam of light down the middle of the image, evoking a detective/investigation or film-noir motif. Centered over the beam is the large bold title in two stacked words: 'CASE' (white/yellow) and 'SPOTLIGHT' (golden-yellow), in a heavy condensed sans-serif. A horizontal divider band runs near the bottom, beneath which sits the DHS seal and the white 'ICE' wordmark. Style is a moody, branded template graphic used to introduce a featured criminal case (here, per the tweet, a 'Santa Claus' impersonator/retired teacher arrested by HSI Newark for child sexual abuse material). The literal flashlight 'spotlight' is a visual pun on highlighting/exposing a case. Messaging technique: branded recurring-series packaging that dramatizes ICE/HSI investigations and frames the agency as shining a light on hidden criminals. Target audience: general social-media public following ICE enforcement content.
